description:
You are the site administrator in charge of the “user-accounts” micro-service that lives in  

        /home/user/user-accounts

    The project uses a *very* small release-management convention:

      • The current semantic version is stored in the text file  
        /home/user/user-accounts/VERSION

      • The project history is kept in  
        /home/user/user-accounts/CHANGELOG.md

    Presently the content of those two files is:

        /home/user/user-accounts/VERSION
        ───────────────────────────────
        1.5.2

        /home/user/user-accounts/CHANGELOG.md
        ─────────────────────────────────────
        ## [1.5.2] - 2023-09-28
        - Improve password hashing

        ## [1.5.1] - 2023-09-20
        - Add MFA enrolment command

        ## [1.5.0] - 2023-09-15
        - Initial public release


    A small cosmetic bug has just been fixed (“Fix typo in account deletion confirmation”).
    Because this is a backwards-compatible bug-fix, you must bump the PATCH part of
    the version string from 1.5.2 → 1.5.3 and add a matching entry at the **top** of
    the changelog.

    Required final state
    --------------------
    1. /home/user/user-accounts/VERSION  
       • must contain exactly the single line  

             1.5.3

    2. /home/user/user-accounts/CHANGELOG.md  
       • the new entry must be placed **before** the existing “## [1.5.2]” section.  
       • The format of the new block must be *precisely*:

             ## [1.5.3] - 2023-10-05
             - Fix typo in account deletion confirmation

       • Make sure there is **one** blank new-line after the bullet line before the
         older 1.5.2 section begins, so automated tests can read it unambiguously.

    3. /home/user/user-accounts/upgrade.log  
       • Append (or create) one single line saying  

             Bumped to 1.5.3 - Fix typo in account deletion confirmation

    Practical constraints
    ---------------------
    • Accomplish everything with **one** terminal command of your choice
      (a shell one-liner is fine).

    • You do *not* have root, so stay inside /home/user.

    • Do not change anything else in the directory tree.

    The grading script will check:

        – exact contents of /home/user/user-accounts/VERSION  
        – presence and correct placement/format of the new 1.5.3 block  
        – exact single-line entry inside /home/user/user-accounts/upgrade.log

    Provide no extra commentary—just execute your single command.
truth:
After successful completion the files must look exactly as shown here (Unix LF line-endings):

    1. /home/user/user-accounts/VERSION
       --------------------------------
       1.5.3\n

    2. /home/user/user-accounts/CHANGELOG.md
       -------------------------------------
       ## [1.5.3] - 2023-10-05\n
       - Fix typo in account deletion confirmation\n
       \n
       ## [1.5.2] - 2023-09-28\n
       - Improve password hashing\n
       \n
       ## [1.5.1] - 2023-09-20\n
       - Add MFA enrolment command\n
       \n
       ## [1.5.0] - 2023-09-15\n
       - Initial public release\n

    3. /home/user/user-accounts/upgrade.log
       ------------------------------------
       Bumped to 1.5.3 - Fix typo in account deletion confirmation\n
